page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 15 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with an x86_64 architecture, the instance operates at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz across 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The CPU configuration includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for matrix multiplication acceleration. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core performance, particularly in text processing and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, as evidenced by lower scores in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, multi-core Geekbench tests, and uncached memory read/write operations. The instance demonstrates average capabilities in static web serving throughput and single-threaded gzip compression.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-standard-4 offers a dedicated hardware profile suitable for workloads that require predictable single-thread CPU execution without the overhead of shared-core environments. However, the resource density and memory bandwidth limitations present a tradeoff for memory-intensive or highly parallelized applications. It is best suited for light web serving, single-threaded data processing, text processing, and small-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-4 is a general-purpose instance configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 15 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 2 physical cores, 2 threads per core, and a 260 MB L3 cache. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, particularly in text processing and single-threaded decompression, though multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are comparatively weak. Qualitatively, the instance provides a dedicated, predictable compute environment for workloads that do not require high memory throughput. It is best suited for single-threaded processing, light web serving, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-4 is a general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance, suitable for text processing and light web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-4 features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 15 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: A dedicated four-vCPU virtual machine delivering strong single-threaded performance and advanced instruction support for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Four dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz
15 GB of system memory with a 260 MB L3 CPU cache
Strong single-core performance in text processing and decompression benchmarks
Support for advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for single-threaded applications, light web serving, and small LLM inference
